Reference to the commission schedule or rate agreement applied to this accrual record. Determines the applicable commission rate tier, calculation method, and contractual terms with the OTA or GDS partner.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission cost-of-acquisition reporting by market segment is a standard revenue management KPI. Accruals currently carry a denormalized market_segment_code; a proper FK to market_segment enables norm

Reference to the OTA or GDS partner entity responsible for the booking. Populated when the channel type is OTA or GDS; null for direct bookings. Supports OTA partnership performance and commission reconciliation reporting.
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the commission is being accrued. Enables property-level commission cost reporting and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) analysis.

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking. Business justification: Commission accruals must link directly to reservations for reconciliation, dispute resolution, cancellation-driven commission reversals, and complete audit trail from booking to financial posting. Cur

Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: OTA/GDS commission accruals for spa treatments booked via distribution channels must reference the originating spa appointment for spa-specific commission reporting, dispute resolution, and channel co

The accounting date on which the commission liability was recognized in the general ledger. Aligns with USALI period-end accrual practices and SAP S/4HANA GL posting date. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.

Current lifecycle state of the commission accrual record. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) workflow in SAP S/4HANA: 'accrued' = liability recognized; 'invoiced' = OTA invoice received; 'paid' = payment disbursed; 'disputed' = under reconciliation review; 'cancelled' = booking cancelled before stay; 'reversed' = accrual reversed due to no-show or refund. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.

The Average Daily Rate of the booking, calculated as gross_booking_value divided by room_nights. Stored for channel performance benchmarking and to support STR STAR Report comparisons without requiring recalculation from source.
The date on which the reservation was cancelled, if applicable. When populated, triggers commission accrual reversal logic per the cancellation policy terms. Null for active or completed bookings.

Classification of the distribution channel through which the booking originated. Drives commission rate tier selection and channel mix reporting. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus); direct_web = brand.com; voice = call center; corporate = negotiated corporate account; wholesale = tour operator/wholesaler.

The commission amount converted to the company's functional base currency (typically USD) for consolidated financial reporting and USALI-compliant P&L statements. Enables cross-property and cross-currency commission cost analysis. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.

The calculated commission amount payable to the distribution channel partner in the local transaction currency (gross_booking_value × commission_rate). Used for OTA invoice reconciliation and AP liability posting. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.

Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: 'room_revenue' = room charges only; 'total_revenue' = all charges including F&B and ancillary; 'net_revenue' = revenue net of taxes and fees; 'flat_fee' = fixed amount per booking regardless of revenue.

The contractual commission rate applied to the gross booking value, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Sourced from the applicable commission schedule. Used to calculate the commission amount and for rate parity and cost-of-acquisition analysis.

Classification of the commission arrangement: 'standard' = default contractual rate; 'override' = manually overridden rate approved by revenue management; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional rate; 'group' = group booking commission; 'negotiated' = bilaterally negotiated rate outside standard schedule.

The GDS or channel connectivity fee charged by the distribution technology provider (e.g., Sabre SynXis transaction fee) associated with this booking, in local currency. Included in total cost-of-acquisition (CoA) reporting alongside commission.

Timestamp when this commission accrual record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and SOX financial controls compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

Free-text notes describing the nature of a commission dispute with the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status is 'disputed'. Supports the channel dispute resolution workflow and AP reconciliation process.

The exchange rate applied to convert the local currency commission amount to the base currency at the time of accrual. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury rates. Required for accurate multi-currency financial reporting and audit trail.

The SAP S/4HANA GL account code to which the commission expense is posted (e.g., USALI Schedule 6 - Sales and Marketing / Distribution Costs). Ensures correct financial statement classification per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.

The total gross booking value of the reservation on which commission is calculated, in the local transaction currency. Represents the full room revenue amount before any discounts or adjustments. Forms the base for commission rate application. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
The date on the commission invoice received from the OTA or GDS partner. Used for AP aging analysis, payment due date calculation, and period-end accrual reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.

The OTA or GDS partner's invoice reference number received for commission payment. Used to match accrued commission liabilities against received invoices in SAP S/4HANA AP for three-way reconciliation. Null until invoice is received.

Indicates whether the booking is eligible for commission payment (True) or is a non-commissionable booking (False), such as direct loyalty redemptions, staff rates, or complimentary stays. Drives commission accrual logic and AP posting.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the booking was made and commission is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency commission reconciliation and FX conversion. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
The actual date on which the commission payment was disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status transitions to 'paid'. Used for AP reconciliation and cash flow reporting.
The contractual due date by which the commission payment must be disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner per the commission schedule terms. Used for AP cash flow planning and SLA compliance monitoring.

The payment transaction reference or bank transfer reference number from SAP S/4HANA AP when the commission is disbursed. Enables end-to-end traceability from accrual to cash payment for audit and reconciliation purposes.

The reason code for reversing a previously accrued commission. Populated when accrual_status is 'reversed' or 'cancelled'. Supports AP reconciliation and channel dispute management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cancellation|no_show|rate_correction|duplicate|dispute_resolved|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]

The number of room nights associated with the booking for which commission is being accrued. Used to calculate CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) and to normalize commission costs across bookings of varying LOS (Length of Stay).

The total distribution cost for this booking, combining commission_amount_local and connectivity_fee_amount. Represents the all-in cost of acquiring the booking through this channel. Used for channel profitability and net RevPAR analysis.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission accrual record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, reconciliation audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
Unique surrogate identifier for each commission or fee schedule record in the channel distribution cost management system. Primary key for the commission_schedule data product.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Commission schedules define the commission terms negotiated in channel contracts with OTA partners and GDS providers. Adding nullable channel_contract_id FK links commission schedules to their governi

Reference to the specific rate plan (BAR, LRA, NRR, package, negotiated corporate) to which this commission schedule is scoped. Null indicates the schedule applies to all rate plans for the channel partner.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Commission schedules define fee structures for specific distribution channels. Many commission schedules per channel (e.g., tiered by volume, rate plan, market segment). Removes redundant channel_type

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Commission schedules are differentiated by market segment (corporate, leisure, consortia). Linking commission_schedule to market_segment normalizes the existing denormalized market_segment_code and su
Reference to the distribution channel partner (OTA, GDS, CRS, channel manager, direct booking engine) to which this commission or fee schedule applies. Links to the channel partner master record.

Reference to the specific property or hotel to which this commission schedule applies. Null or a portfolio-level sentinel value indicates the schedule applies across all properties (chain-wide agreement).

Indicates whether the commission or fee is still payable when a booking is cancelled (True = commission owed on cancellations, False = commission waived on cancellations). Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) commission accrual and cancellation cost management.

Indicates whether the commission or fee is payable on no-show bookings (True = commission owed on no-shows, False = commission waived). Affects no-show revenue net-of-commission calculations and OTA contract compliance.
Name or identifier of the authorized approver (e.g., VP Revenue Management, CFO) who approved this commission schedule for execution. Required for SOX financial controls compliance and audit trail.
Date and time when this commission schedule was formally approved for activation. Part of the SOX-compliant approval audit trail for financial commitments.

Indicates whether commission and fee accruals under this schedule are automatically calculated and posted to the GL upon booking confirmation (True) or require manual accrual processing (False). Supports automated AP accrual workflows in SAP S/4HANA.

Frequency at which the channel partner invoices the property for commissions or fees: monthly (most common for OTA commissions), weekly, per_booking (real-time deduction), quarterly, annual (common for connectivity subscription fees). Drives AP accrual scheduling and cash flow forecasting.

Maximum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction. Used in conjunction with booking_window_min_days for last-minute rate commission scoping.

Minimum advance booking window (in days before arrival) for which this commission schedule is applicable. Used for LOS (Length of Stay) and advance purchase rate plan commission scoping. Null indicates no minimum booking window restriction.

The unit basis on which the commission or fee is calculated and accrued: per_booking (one charge per reservation), per_room_night (charge per occupied room night), per_segment (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction (CRS per-transaction charge), per_period (fixed charge per billing period regardless of volume). Drives accrual engine logic.

Negotiated commission rate expressed as a percentage of the applicable revenue base (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Applicable when fee_structure is 'percentage'. Represents the contractually agreed OTA or agent commission rate. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
Date and time when this commission schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.

The calendar date from which this commission or fee schedule becomes contractually binding and applicable to bookings. Used to determine which schedule version applies to a given booking date for accrual and invoice reconciliation.

The calendar date on which this commission or fee schedule expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended agreement with no defined expiry. Used for schedule versioning and contract renewal tracking.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the flat fee or tiered threshold amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP reconciliation and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Defines how the commission or fee is calculated: percentage (applied as a % of booking revenue or room revenue), flat (fixed monetary amount per booking or period), tiered (rate varies by volume or revenue thresholds), per_unit (fixed amount per room-night, segment, or transaction unit). Determines the accrual calculation method.

Classification of the cost type captured by this schedule: commission (percentage of booking revenue paid to OTA/agent), per_segment_fee (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction_fee (CRS per-booking transaction fee), connectivity_fee (channel manager or switch connectivity charge), subscription_fee (fixed periodic platform fee), override_commission (negotiated override above standard rate). Drives AP coding and cost-of-acquisition (COA) categorization.

Fixed monetary fee amount per booking, segment, transaction, or billing period, expressed in the schedule currency. Applicable when fee_structure is 'flat' or 'per_unit'. Used for GDS per-segment fees, CRS per-transaction fees, and connectivity subscription fees. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA to which commission and fee expenses are posted for financial reporting under USALI. Ensures correct P&L classification of distribution costs (e.g., OTA commission expense, GDS fee expense) per USALI departmental accounting.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this commission schedule to apply. Null indicates no LOS restriction. Supports commission scoping for extended-stay or minimum-stay rate plans.

Contractual cap on the commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period. Applicable for schedules with a commission ceiling clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Contractual minimum commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period, regardless of the calculated percentage amount. Applicable for schedules with a commission floor clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Name or identifier of the internal team or individual (e.g., Revenue Management, Channel Distribution, Procurement) responsible for negotiating this commission schedule. Used for accountability tracking and contract ownership.

Free-text field for capturing additional context, special contractual conditions, negotiation history notes, or exceptions associated with this commission schedule. Not used for structured data; intended for operational reference by Revenue Management and AP teams.

Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment to the channel partner is due (e.g., 30 = Net 30). Used in AP aging analysis and cash flow management per SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.

Indicates whether rate parity compliance is contractually required and actively monitored for bookings under this commission schedule (True = rate parity clause in effect). Supports rate parity monitoring workflows and OTA contract compliance management.

Defines the revenue component on which the commission percentage is calculated: room_revenue (room charges only), total_revenue (all charges including F&B and ancillaries), net_revenue (after discounts and taxes), gross_revenue (before deductions), f_and_b_revenue (food and beverage charges only). Critical for accurate commission accrual and invoice reconciliation.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this commission or fee schedule, used in AP reconciliation, contract references, and channel partner communications (e.g., OTA-EXP-COMM-2024, GDS-SAB-SEG-FEE).

Human-readable descriptive name for the commission or fee schedule, used in reporting dashboards, AP workflows, and channel profitability analysis (e.g., 'Expedia Standard OTA Commission FY2024', 'Sabre GDS Per-Segment Fee Q1 2024').

Current lifecycle state of the commission schedule: active (currently in force), inactive (temporarily suspended), pending (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective_until date), terminated (cancelled before expiry), draft (under negotiation, not yet approved). Drives AP processing eligibility and accrual inclusion.
Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 1 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Lower volume or revenue threshold (in booking count or currency amount) that triggers the Tier 1 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 2 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Upper volume or revenue threshold that triggers the Tier 2 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
Date and time when this commission schedule record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory allocation record in the channel distribution system. Primary key for the inventory_allocation data product.
Reference to the rate plan associated with this inventory allocation. Determines which pricing rules apply to the allocated inventory on this channel.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Group room block inventory allocated through a channel must reference the event booking to enable pickup tracking, release management, and overbooking controls against the contracted group block — a f
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this inventory allocation applies. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel configuration.
Reference to the room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe double, suite) for which inventory is being allocated to the channel.
Reference to the property for which inventory is being allocated. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Foreign key linking to revenue.rate_restriction. Business justification: RMS-driven channel inventory allocations (stop-sell, CTA, CTD, min/max LOS) are directly triggered by rate restrictions. Linking allocation records to the governing rate_restriction supports audit tra

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_block. Business justification: Channel allotment/group allocation management: when inventory is allocated to a channel from a contracted room block, the allocation record must reference the originating block for pickup tracking, at

The type of inventory control action that generated this record. CREATE indicates a new allocation was established; UPDATE modifies an existing allocation; CLOSE applies a stop-sell or close-out; REOPEN lifts a restriction; RELEASE returns unsold inventory to the pool; OVERRIDE applies a manual revenue management override.
Current operational status of this channel inventory allocation. Active allocations are enforced by the channel management system.
The total number of room nights (units) assigned to this channel for the specified stay date range and room type. Represents the allotment block size or sell limit granted to the channel.

Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) to uniquely identify this inventory allocation action. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.

Current lifecycle state of the inventory allocation record. ACTIVE indicates the allocation is live and controlling channel availability; INACTIVE means it has been superseded; EXPIRED means the stay date range has passed; CANCELLED means it was manually revoked; PENDING means it is awaiting activation.
Method by which inventory is allocated to this channel for this room type. POOLED = shared inventory pool, ALLOCATED = fixed allocation, FREESALE = unlimited access, BLOCKED = no access.

Classification of the inventory allotment method applied to this channel. FREE_SELL allows the channel to sell without a fixed block; ALLOCATION assigns a fixed block of rooms; STOP_SELL halts sales on the channel; CLOSE_OUT removes all availability; ON_REQUEST requires manual confirmation before booking is confirmed.
The username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system user who applied this inventory control action. Used for audit trail, accountability, and change management reporting.

The number of room nights currently available for sale on this channel, calculated as allocated_units minus consumed_units. Represents the real-time sellable inventory for the channel on the specified stay dates.
The number of bookings cancelled on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Used to track net pickup and assess channel-level cancellation patterns for demand forecasting.
Percentage of total room type inventory allocated to this channel. Used for allocation-based inventory management strategies.

The category of distribution channel for this allocation. OTA (Online Travel Agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), DIRECT_WEB (brand.com), VOICE (call center), GSO (Global Sales Office), WHOLESALE (tour operators/wholesalers), METASEARCH (Google, Trivago, etc.). Supports channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|gso|wholesale|metasearch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.15 for 15%) payable to the distribution channel for bookings made under this allocation. Used for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.

The fixed or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the GDS or CRS for distributing inventory through this channel. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for total cost of distribution analysis.
The number of allocated room nights that have been booked or consumed by the channel as of the last system update. Used to calculate remaining availability and monitor channel pickup performance.
Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.
The date and time when this inventory allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.

The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial fields (connectivity_fee) in this allocation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.

The number of days between the allocation action date and the stay_date_from (booking window). Used in revenue management analysis to understand how far in advance inventory controls are being applied relative to the arrival date.
Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration expires. Null for currently active allocations.
Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration becomes effective.

The date and time when this inventory allocation action became effective and was applied to the channel. Represents the business event timestamp when the allocation, stop-sell, or restriction was activated in the CRS.

Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the first date of the stay period for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check in on the stay_date_from date, preventing new bookings that start on that date.
Indicates whether departures are blocked on the stay_date_to date for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check out on that date, preventing bookings that end on that date.

Indicates whether this inventory allocation action was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) as opposed to being manually entered by a revenue manager. Supports analysis of automation adoption and RMS recommendation acceptance rates.

Indicates whether a stop-sell control has been applied to this channel for the specified stay dates and room type. When TRUE, the channel is prevented from accepting new bookings regardless of remaining available units.
Timestamp when this allocation record was last updated.

Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is contractually required for this channel. When TRUE, the channel must be offered the last available room at the property, typically a contractual obligation with certain OTA or corporate accounts.
The date and time when this inventory allocation action was lifted, reversed, or superseded. Null if the allocation is still active. Used to track the full lifecycle of each inventory control action.
Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room-type-channel combination, overriding property-level LRA settings.
The maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MAX_LOS. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction is applied.
Channel-specific maximum length of stay restriction for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.
The minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MIN_LOS. A value of 1 means no effective minimum restriction.
Channel-specific minimum length of stay requirement for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.

The number of additional room nights above the physical inventory that this channel is permitted to sell as part of the property's overbooking strategy. A value of 0 means no overbooking is permitted on this channel.
Maximum overbooking percentage allowed for this room type on this channel. Used for revenue management optimization.
The number of new bookings received on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Supports pickup reporting and demand forecasting in the Revenue Management System (RMS).
Priority ranking for this channel when allocating inventory for this room type. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Indicates whether rate parity rules are actively enforced for this room type on this channel.

Indicates whether rate parity compliance is enforced for this channel allocation. When TRUE, the allocated inventory must be offered at rates consistent with other channels per rate parity agreements. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.

Standardized code identifying the business reason for the inventory control action (e.g., HIGH_DEMAND, GROUP_BLOCK, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, MAINTENANCE). Used for revenue management analysis and audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HIGH_DEMAND|GROUP_BLOCK|OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION|RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION|CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE|MAINTENANCE|FORECAST_ADJUSTMENT|COMPETITIVE_RESPONSE — promote to reference product]

Free-text narrative providing additional context for the inventory control action beyond the standardized reason_code. Captures specific business justification entered by the revenue manager or system.

The date on which any unsold allocated inventory is released back to the general property inventory pool. After this date, the channel no longer holds exclusive rights to the allocated units, enabling the property to sell them through other channels.

The type of booking restriction applied to this inventory allocation. MIN_LOS enforces a minimum Length of Stay (LOS); MAX_LOS enforces a maximum LOS; CLOSED_TO_ARRIVAL (CTA) prevents new arrivals on the specified date; CLOSED_TO_DEPARTURE (CTD) prevents departures; FULL_PATTERN_LENGTH requires exact stay duration; NONE means no restriction is applied.

The maximum number of room nights the channel is permitted to sell regardless of total property availability. Acts as a cap to prevent overselling on a specific channel and supports channel mix optimization.
The first date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the start of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
The last date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the end of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
The date and time when this inventory allocation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the allocation for audit trail and change management purposes.

Monotonically incrementing version counter for this inventory allocation record. Incremented each time the allocation is modified, enabling optimistic concurrency control and historical version tracking in the Silver layer.
Primary key for channel

Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Many distribution channels are operated by OTA partners (e.g., Expedia operates multiple channels/brands). This FK establishes the ownership relationship. Removes redundant platform_name which can be

Date on which this distribution channel was first activated and began accepting live bookings. Distinct from contract_start_date which reflects the commercial agreement date. Used for channel onboarding tracking and performance benchmarking from go-live.

Indicates whether this channel is eligible to display and sell the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate at the time of booking. True = BAR rates are distributed to this channel; False = channel receives only specific contracted or negotiated rates. Drives rate distribution strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Per-transaction booking fee charged by the channel in addition to or instead of a commission percentage, expressed in USD. Common in GDS channels (e.g., GDS segment fee per booking). Used in cost-of-acquisition and channel profitability analysis.

Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel (e.g., '24H_FREE', '48H_PENALTY', 'NON_REFUNDABLE'). Cancellation policies may differ by channel based on contractual agreements. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

High-level grouping of the channel by booking relationship: DIRECT = guest books directly with the property (web, mobile, voice, corporate direct); INDIRECT = third-party intermediary involved (OTA, GDS, wholesaler, metasearch); OPAQUE = rate and property identity concealed until after booking (e.g., Hotwire, Priceline opaque). Drives cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel mix strategy.

Current operational lifecycle status of the distribution channel. active = channel is live and accepting bookings; inactive = channel is configured but not currently selling; suspended = channel temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity violation, connectivity issue); pending_activation = channel onboarded but not yet live; decommissioned = channel permanently retired.

Categorical classification of the distribution channel by its market segment and technology interface. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo); DIRECT_WEB = brand.com web booking; DIRECT_MOBILE = brand mobile app; VOICE = call center reservations; CORPORATE_DIRECT = negotiated corporate accounts; WHOLESALER = tour operators and bed banks; METASEARCH = Google Hotel Ads, Trivago. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|DIRECT_WEB|DIRECT_MOBILE|VOICE|CORPORATE_DIRECT|WHOLESALER|METASEARCH — promote to reference product]

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the distribution channel across enterprise systems including Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and IDeaS G3 RMS. Used for channel mapping and cross-system reconciliation. [DQ: denormalized natural key flagged for normalization to SSOT FK]

The commercial model under which commission is calculated for this channel. NET_RATE = channel marks up a net rate provided by the property; GROSS_RATE = commission deducted from the gross rate charged to the guest; MARKUP = channel applies a markup over the property's net rate; FLAT_FEE = fixed fee per booking regardless of rate. Critical for accurate revenue and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Standard commission percentage charged by this distribution channel on each booking, expressed as a percentage of the room revenue (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Drives cost-of-acquisition calculations and net revenue reporting per USALI standards. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Annual or periodic connectivity/subscription fee charged by the channel or switch provider for maintaining the technical integration, expressed in USD. Included in total cost-of-acquisition analysis alongside commission. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Technical integration method used to connect the property management system or CRS to this distribution channel. API = RESTful or SOAP API integration; XML = XML-based messaging (e.g., OTA XML); SFTP = file-based batch transfer; DIRECT_CONNECT = proprietary direct connection; SWITCH = via a channel manager switch (e.g., SiteMinder, Channex); MANUAL = manually managed extranet updates. Impacts SLA and rate update latency.

Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities, policies) is actively distributed to this channel via a content API or content management system. True = content is pushed/synced to this channel; False = content is managed manually or not distributed. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels.

Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal management and channel lifecycle planning. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission audit trails, and regulatory compliance. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

Timestamp when this distribution channel record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.

Channel identifier as configured in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the CRS source of record. Enables traceability of bookings back to the originating CRS channel configuration. [DQ: denormalized natural key flagged for normalization to SSOT FK]

Date on which this distribution channel was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active channels. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting, and audit trails. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

Free-text description of the distribution channel, including its market positioning, target guest segment, special contractual terms, or operational notes. Used for channel management documentation and onboarding reference.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processor Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel acts as a GDPR data processor for guest personal data collected during the booking process. True = a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with this channel; False = channel does not process personal data on behalf of the property. Required for GDPR compliance and data governance.

Two-letter GDS chain code assigned to the hotel brand or property group within Global Distribution Systems (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Required for GDS-type channels to route availability and rate requests correctly. Null for non-GDS channels.

Geographic reach of this distribution channel. GLOBAL = channel operates worldwide; REGIONAL = channel serves a specific geographic region (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe); DOMESTIC = channel serves a single country; LOCAL = channel serves a specific city or metro area. Used for channel mix strategy and market segmentation analysis.

Method by which room inventory is allocated to this distribution channel. POOLED = channel draws from the shared central inventory pool (real-time); ALLOTMENT = a fixed block of rooms is pre-allocated to the channel; FREE_SALE = channel can sell without prior allocation up to a defined limit; ON_REQUEST = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether this channel has Last Room Availability (LRA) rights — meaning the property must make the last available room accessible through this channel at the contracted rate. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. True = LRA applies; False = LRA does not apply.

Timestamp when this distribution channel record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.

Indicates whether bookings made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and redemption. True = loyalty points are awarded and redeemable; False = bookings through this channel do not qualify for loyalty benefits (common for opaque OTA channels). Managed in Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.

business_glossary_term: Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE) Bookings Supported Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel supports group and MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) bookings in addition to transient reservations. True = MICE/group bookings can be sourced through this channel; False = transient bookings only. Relevant for event sales and Delphi by Amadeus integration.

Human-readable name of the distribution channel (e.g., 'Expedia Partner Central', 'Booking.com Extranet', 'Sabre GDS', 'Direct Web', 'Voice Reservations'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.

Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) products are distributed through this channel. True = NRR fences are available on this channel; False = only flexible/refundable rates are distributed. Impacts revenue management yield strategy and cancellation policy enforcement.

Defines who collects payment from the guest for bookings made through this channel. HOTEL_COLLECT = property collects payment directly at check-in/check-out; CHANNEL_COLLECT = the OTA or intermediary collects payment and remits net to the property; SPLIT = partial payment collected by each party. Impacts accounts receivable and revenue recognition per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.

Indicates whether this distribution channel is certified as compliant with PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) for handling cardholder data. True = channel holds current PCI DSS certification; False = channel is not PCI compliant or certification has lapsed. Required for risk management and payment security governance.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market served by this distribution channel (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for geographic channel mix analysis, market penetration reporting, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU-based channels).

Numeric rank indicating the strategic priority of this channel in the distribution mix, where lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority, typically direct web). Used by revenue management to determine inventory release sequencing and rate strategy. Configured in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether this distribution channel contractually requires rate parity — i.e., the property must offer the same or lower rates on this channel as on any other channel. True = rate parity clause is active in the channel agreement; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management compliance and rate strategy.

Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation for this channel. BROAD = property must match rates across all channels including direct; NARROW = property must match rates on other OTAs but may offer lower rates on direct channels (common post-EU regulatory changes); NONE = no rate parity obligation. Drives revenue management and direct booking strategy.

Frequency at which the channel remits collected payments to the property for CHANNEL_COLLECT payment model bookings. Applicable only when payment_model is CHANNEL_COLLECT or SPLIT. Drives accounts receivable forecasting and cash flow management in SAP S/4HANA.

business_glossary_term: Channel Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time (Seconds)
description: Maximum allowable response time in seconds for availability and rate queries from this channel, as defined in the channel's Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used to monitor channel connectivity performance and enforce SLA compliance. Typical values: 3 seconds for OTA/GDS, 5 seconds for XML channels.

business_glossary_term: Channel Service Level Agreement (SLA) Uptime Percentage
description: Contractually committed uptime percentage for this channel's connectivity, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.90 for 99.9% uptime). Used to monitor channel reliability and enforce SLA penalties. Managed against actual uptime metrics in channel performance reporting.

Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes supported by this distribution channel for booking and payment (e.g., 'USD,EUR,GBP,JPY'). Relevant for multi-currency rate distribution and foreign exchange management. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS.

Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 two-letter language codes supported by this channel for content display and guest communication (e.g., 'en,fr,de,zh,ja'). Relevant for content localization strategy and international market reach.
Unique surrogate identifier for the channel contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, etc.) that is party to this contract.

Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: Channel contracts may reference specific GDS connection configurations, particularly for chain-level GDS agreements that are implemented at the property connection level. Many contracts per GDS connec

Foreign key linking to channel.ota_partner. Business justification: Channel contracts are commercial agreements signed with OTA partners, defining commission rates, rate parity obligations, and terms. Many contracts per OTA partner (e.g., master agreement, property-sp
Reference to the property or portfolio of properties covered by this contract. Null if the contract applies at the brand or enterprise level.
Indicates whether the channel is contractually entitled to receive the Best Available Rate (BAR) as defined in the Revenue Management System. Drives rate loading rules in Sabre SynXis CRS.
Code referencing the contracted cancellation and no-show policy applicable to bookings made through this channel (e.g., '24H', '48H', 'NON_REF'). Loaded into Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: net rate (after discounts), gross rate (before discounts), room revenue only, or total revenue including F&B and ancillaries.

Base commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 = 15%) payable to the channel partner per confirmed booking under this contract. Distinct from the commission_schedule product which captures tiered or conditional rate structures.

Fixed or per-transaction fee charged by the channel or GDS for technical connectivity and distribution services. Distinct from commission which is percentage-based. Used in cost-of-acquisition (COA) analysis.
Specifies how the connectivity fee is structured: per booking transaction, per GDS segment, annual flat fee, or monthly flat fee.

Free-text description of contractual obligations regarding property content (images, descriptions, amenities) that must be maintained on the channel platform, including minimum image counts, refresh frequency, and accuracy standards.
Human-readable descriptive name for the contract (e.g., 'Expedia 2025 Master Distribution Agreement — Americas').
Externally-known legal contract reference number assigned by the contracting parties or the legal department. Used for correspondence, audits, and dispute resolution.
Current lifecycle state of the contract. Drives whether the channel is eligible to receive inventory and rates via the CRS.

Classification of the commercial agreement by channel category. Determines applicable commission structures, rate parity obligations, and connectivity requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota_distribution|gds_participation|direct_connect|wholesale|bed_bank|metasearch|corporate_negotiated — promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system of record. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values in this contract are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Indicates whether the contract includes a data sharing or data processing agreement governing the exchange of guest and booking data with the channel partner. Required for GDPR Article 28 compliance.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal jurisdiction governing dispute resolution and contract interpretation.
Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the hotel and the channel partner.
Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the channel is authorized to distribute inventory. Aligns with USALI period reporting.
Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and rate parity audits.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Reference
description: Reference number or document identifier for the GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed with the channel partner. Mandatory for EU-based OTA and GDS partnerships under GDPR Article 28.
Frequency at which commission invoices or settlement statements are issued between the hotel and the channel partner.

Indicates whether the contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the hotel to make the last available room accessible to this channel even when inventory is restricted for other channels.

Annual or contract-period monetary commitment for co-operative marketing spend with the channel partner (e.g., sponsored listings, display advertising on OTA platform). Expressed in the contract currency.
Indicates whether the contract permits the hotel to offer Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) through this channel. Relevant for revenue management strategy and cancellation policy governance.

Commercial payment model governing how the channel collects and remits payment: merchant model (channel collects from guest and remits net), agency model (hotel collects and pays commission), net rate (channel marks up a net rate), or wholesale.

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry (PCI) Compliance Confirmed Indicator
description: Indicates whether the channel partner has confirmed PCI DSS compliance as required by the contract terms for handling payment card data.

Tier designation within the OTA or GDS preferred partner program (e.g., Expedia Preferred Plus, Booking.com Preferred). Higher tiers typically confer better placement, lower commission rates, or co-op marketing benefits.

Indicates whether the contract includes a rate parity obligation requiring the hotel to offer the channel the same or better rates as other distribution channels. Critical for revenue management compliance and OTA relationship governance.

Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation: broad parity (match all channels including direct), narrow parity (match only other OTAs, not direct), or no parity obligation. Reflects EU regulatory distinctions post-2015 investigations.
Number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives automated contract renewal alerts.
Specifies whether the contract auto-renews at expiration, requires manual renegotiation, or is a one-time non-renewable agreement.
Date on which all parties executed the contract. Used for legal audit trails and SOX compliance.
Actual date the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Distinct from expiration_date which is the scheduled end. Populated only when contract_status is 'terminated'.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each channel-specific rate plan configuration record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Each channel rate plan (non-refundable OTA, flexible direct, advance purchase) is governed by a specific cancellation policy. This FK normalizes cancellation_policy_code and enables cancellation polic

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Rate plans are defined and loaded within the context of a channel contract — the contract specifies the commercial framework (commission basis, parity obligations, rate loading protocols) under which

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Each channel-specific rate plan configuration belongs to one distribution channel. Many rate plans per channel. Removes redundant channel_code, channel_name, and channel_type which can be retrieved vi

Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Meal-inclusive rate plans (breakfast, half-board, full-board) bundle a specific F&B menu. Revenue managers and F&B controllers need this link to cost meal inclusions per channel rate plan, reconcile F
Identifier of the hotel property for which this channel rate plan configuration applies. Enables property-level rate plan and channel management across a multi-property portfolio.
add column revenue_rate_plan_id (BIGINT) with FK to revenue.revenue_rate_plan.revenue_rate_plan_id - channel rate plans must derive from the revenue master rate plan to resolve SSOT duplicate.

Minimum number of days in advance of arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on this channel. For example, a value of 7 means the guest must book at least 7 days before check-in. Supports advance purchase rate strategies and demand management.

Name or identifier of the revenue management or distribution team member who approved this channel rate plan configuration for publication. Supports governance, approval workflow tracking, and audit compliance for rate distribution decisions.

The base room rate amount (in property currency) from which the channel-specific rate is derived. For BAR-derived plans, this is the current BAR value. For flat rates, this is the fixed rate. Expressed per room per night. Supports rate parity monitoring and channel pricing strategy analysis.

The last calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the closing of the booking window. Nullable if the rate plan remains bookable indefinitely until the stay date.

The earliest calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the opening of the booking window for this rate plan on this channel. Used for promotional and advance purchase rate plan management.

The final channel-specific rate amount (in property currency) published to this distribution channel after applying the rate derivation method and adjustment. This is the rate visible to consumers on the channel. Per room per night. Core metric for rate parity compliance monitoring.

Current lifecycle status of the channel rate plan configuration. Active = currently published and bookable on the channel; Inactive = deactivated and not available; Suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., rate parity violation); Pending = awaiting activation or approval; Expired = past effective end date; Draft = configured but not yet submitted for distribution.

Number of days before arrival after which this rate plan is closed out and no longer bookable on this channel. Complements advance_purchase_days to define the booking window. A value of 0 means the rate is bookable up to the day of arrival.

The commission percentage payable to the distribution channel (e.g., OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings made under this rate plan. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Critical for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.

Fixed fee amount (in property currency) charged by the channel or connectivity provider per booking or per period for distributing this rate plan on the channel. Distinct from commission (which is percentage-based). Supports total cost of acquisition calculation.
The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Rate Plan ID
description: The rate plan identifier as assigned in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRS. Enables traceability of rate plan configurations back to the source system.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rate amounts on this channel rate plan. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, AED. Supports multi-currency channel distribution and rate parity analysis across international markets.

A 7-character binary string indicating which days of the week (Mon-Sun) this rate plan is applicable on the channel. Each position represents a day: position 1=Monday through position 7=Sunday. '1' = applicable, '0' = not applicable. Example: '1111100' = Monday through Friday only. Supports weekday/weekend rate differentiation.

The date from which this channel rate plan configuration becomes active and the rate plan is published to the channel. Defines the start of the rate loading schedule for this channel-rate plan combination.

The date on which this channel rate plan configuration expires and the rate plan is no longer published to the channel. Nullable for open-ended configurations. Supports rate loading schedule management and seasonal rate plan publishing.

Indicates whether this channel rate plan is a package rate that bundles room accommodation with additional inclusions (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, F&B voucher). True = package rate with inclusions; False = room-only rate. Supports package revenue attribution and channel mix analysis.

Indicates whether rate parity rules apply to this channel rate plan configuration. True = this rate plan is subject to rate parity monitoring and must match rates across applicable channels; False = exempt from parity (e.g., opaque rates, member-only rates, negotiated corporate rates). Supports rate parity compliance management.

Indicates whether bookings made under this channel rate plan are eligible for a refund upon cancellation. True = refundable (flexible rate); False = non-refundable (NRR). Key attribute for consumer transparency and rate plan classification.

The date and time when this rate plan was most recently successfully loaded or updated on the distribution channel. Supports rate loading schedule management, troubleshooting, and audit of channel rate distribution operations.

Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under this rate plan on this channel. Nullable if no maximum stay restriction applies. Used in conjunction with min_los to define the eligible stay window for this channel rate plan.

Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this channel rate plan. RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board (breakfast + dinner); FB = Full Board (all meals); AI = All Inclusive; EP = European Plan (no meals); CP = Continental Plan (continental breakfast). Relevant for package rate plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RO|BB|HB|FB|AI|EP|CP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this rate plan on this channel. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions are a key Revenue Management System (RMS) tool for yield optimization.

Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this channel rate plan configuration. Examples: rate loading exceptions, channel-specific negotiation notes, promotional campaign references, or revenue strategy rationale.

business_glossary_term: Property Management System (PMS) Rate Plan Code
description: The rate plan code as configured in Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for reconciliation between CRS channel distribution and PMS rate plan setup. Ensures rate plan consistency between the CRS and PMS for accurate reservation processing.

Indicates whether the channel-specific pricing adjustment is a markup (rate higher than base, e.g., to cover OTA commission) or a markdown (rate lower than base, e.g., promotional discount). NONE indicates no adjustment is applied and the base rate is published as-is.

Specifies whether the rate_adjustment_value is expressed as a PERCENTAGE of the base rate or as a fixed AMOUNT in the property currency. Determines how the channel-specific rate is computed from the base rate and adjustment value.

The numeric value of the rate adjustment applied to derive the channel-specific rate from the base rate. Interpretation depends on rate_adjustment_unit: if PERCENTAGE, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%); if AMOUNT, this is the absolute currency amount. Used in conjunction with rate_adjustment_type to compute the final channel rate.

The method used to calculate the channel-specific rate from the base rate. FLAT = fixed absolute rate amount; PERCENTAGE = percentage markup or markdown applied to a base rate; BAR_DERIVED = derived as a percentage or offset from the Best Available Rate (BAR); YIELD_MANAGED = dynamically set by the Revenue Management System (RMS); NEGOTIATED_FIXED = fixed contracted rate for corporate or wholesale agreements.

Operational status of the rate plan loading process to the channel. Loaded = successfully transmitted and active on the channel; Pending Load = queued for transmission to the channel; Load Failed = transmission error requiring remediation; Not Loaded = not yet submitted; Deactivated = previously loaded but now removed from the channel.

Code identifying the rate parity group to which this channel rate plan belongs. Rate plans within the same parity group are monitored for price consistency across channels. Enables grouping of comparable rate plans for parity compliance reporting.

The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as configured in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Examples: BAR, NRR, LRA, CORP001, PKG-BB. This is the business identifier used across Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate loading and distribution.

Human-readable descriptive name of the rate plan as displayed in the CRS and channel interfaces. Examples: 'Best Available Rate', 'Non-Refundable Rate', 'Last Room Availability Corporate', 'Bed and Breakfast Package'.

Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR = Best Available Rate (dynamic public rate); NRR = Non-Refundable Rate (discounted, no cancellation); LRA = Last Room Availability (guaranteed availability for corporate); CORPORATE = negotiated corporate rate; PACKAGE = bundled rate with inclusions; PROMOTIONAL = limited-time promotional rate; WHOLESALE = net rate for tour operators; NEGOTIATED = contracted rate; OPAQUE = channel-opaque pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|NRR|LRA|CORPORATE|PACKAGE|PROMOTIONAL|WHOLESALE|NEGOTIATED|OPAQUE — promote to reference product]

Comma-separated list of room type codes to which this channel rate plan applies. If null or 'ALL', the rate plan applies to all room types at the property. Examples: 'STD,DLX,STE' or 'ALL'. Supports room-type-specific channel pricing strategies.

The latest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings must have an arrival date on or before this date. Nullable for open-ended rate plans. Supports seasonal and promotional rate plan management.

The earliest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings made under this rate plan must have an arrival date on or after this date. Supports seasonal rate plan publishing and blackout date management.
The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Unique surrogate identifier for each GDS connection configuration record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master configuration entity.
Reference to the parent distribution channel record associated with this GDS connection, enabling channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.
Reference to the property for which this GDS connection is configured. Links the GDS connectivity record to the specific hotel or resort property.
Date on which this GDS connection was first activated and made live for bookings. Used for contract term tracking, connectivity health history, and channel distribution reporting.

Method by which room availability is synchronized between the property management system and the GDS. Real-time provides instant updates; batch updates on a scheduled interval; on_request queries availability at booking time; seamless provides live two-way availability.

Two- to four-character hotel chain code registered with the GDS network, identifying the hotel brand or management company within the GDS. Assigned by the GDS provider and used for inventory and rate loading.

Percentage commission payable to travel agents or GDS intermediaries on bookings made through this GDS connection. Used for commission accrual, accounts payable processing, and channel cost analysis per USALI standards.

Current operational lifecycle status of the GDS connection. Active indicates live booking capability; suspended indicates temporary halt; pending_activation indicates setup in progress; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.

Fixed monthly fee charged by the GDS provider or switch vendor for maintaining the technical connectivity of this GDS connection. Distinct from per-segment fees; used for OpEx budgeting and channel cost allocation.

Technical connectivity model used for this GDS connection. Direct connect provides real-time two-way communication; switch connect routes through a switch provider; seamless provides full availability and rate transparency; standard uses batch updates.
Expiration date of the GDS connectivity contract. Null for evergreen contracts. Used for renewal alerts, contract renegotiation planning, and channel distribution continuity management.
Effective start date of the GDS connectivity contract governing this connection. Used for contract lifecycle management, renewal planning, and financial obligation tracking.

Indicates whether negotiated corporate rates are loaded and accessible through this GDS connection. Corporate rates are a primary revenue stream for GDS channels and require specific rate plan configuration in the CRS.
Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.

Identifier for the CRS-to-GDS interface configuration within Sabre SynXis CRS. Links the GDS connection to the specific CRS interface instance managing rate distribution and availability synchronization.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are loaded and displayed to travel agents in this GDS connection. Ensures rate accuracy and currency consistency for international GDS distribution.
Date on which this GDS connection was or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for currently active connections. Used for contract lifecycle management and channel distribution planning.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the property's display order in GDS search results for travel agents. Lower values indicate higher priority. Influenced by GDS tier, participation level, and performance metrics.
Human-readable name of the GDS provider (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan, Travelport). Used for reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.
Industry-standard two-character GDS network identifier assigned by IATA/ARC. 1S=Sabre, 1A=Amadeus, 1G=Galileo/Travelport, 1P=Worldspan. Used for routing and segment fee attribution.

Participation tier level for this GDS connection, reflecting the level of GDS merchandising, display priority, and content features contracted. Preferred tier provides enhanced display and content capabilities; standard is base participation; basic is minimal listing.

Result of the most recent automated health check for this GDS connection. Healthy indicates full two-way connectivity; degraded indicates partial functionality; failed indicates connectivity loss; unknown indicates health check has not been performed.

business_glossary_term: Last GDS Connectivity Health Check Timestamp
description: Timestamp of the most recent automated connectivity health check performed for this GDS connection. Used for SLA monitoring, connectivity uptime reporting, and proactive issue detection.

Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this GDS connection, requiring the property to make the last available room accessible to GDS bookers. LRA is a contractual obligation with some GDS providers and corporate accounts.

Maximum number of days in advance that reservations can be booked through this GDS connection. Controls the booking window loaded to the GDS and aligns with the Revenue Management System (RMS) inventory control horizon.

Minimum number of days in advance that reservations must be booked through this GDS connection. Supports Last Room Availability (LRA) and advance purchase rate controls managed by the Revenue Management System.

Free-text operational notes regarding this GDS connection, including special configuration requirements, known issues, contractual exceptions, or migration history. Used by channel distribution and revenue management teams.

Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) can be loaded and booked through this GDS connection. NRR support is a key revenue management capability enabling advance purchase rate strategies via GDS.
The unique property identifier as registered within the specific GDS network. May differ from the internal PMS property code. Used for inventory display and booking routing within the GDS.
GDS-assigned Pseudo City Code (PCC) that identifies the travel agency or booking office within the GDS network. Used for commission tracking, segment attribution, and agency relationship management.

The technical protocol or standard used to load and update rate plans into the GDS. HTNG and OTA are XML-based standards; DHISCO is a switch-based protocol; manual indicates rates are loaded via GDS terminal; API indicates direct programmatic loading.

Indicates whether rate parity is contractually enforced for this GDS connection, requiring that rates loaded to the GDS match the Best Available Rate (BAR) offered on direct channels. Critical for OTA and GDS contract compliance monitoring.
Number of distinct rate plans currently loaded and active for this GDS connection. Used to monitor rate plan coverage, identify gaps in GDS rate distribution, and support rate parity audits.

Frequency at which rate updates are pushed from the Revenue Management System (RMS) or CRS to this GDS connection. Real-time supports dynamic pricing; scheduled frequencies reflect batch update configurations.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the GDS segment fee. Enables multi-currency cost-of-acquisition reporting for properties operating in non-USD markets.

Per-booking segment fee charged by the GDS provider in US dollars for each reservation booked through this GDS connection. A GDS segment is one hotel booking transaction. Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.
Email address for the GDS provider's technical support team for this connection. Used for connectivity issue escalation, health check alerts, and SLA breach notifications.

Name of the third-party switch provider (e.g., DHISCO, TravelClick, Pegasus) used to route availability and rate data between the CRS and the GDS, when connectivity_type is switch_connect. Null for direct connect configurations.
Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance in the silver layer lakehouse.

business_glossary_term: GDS Connectivity Uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) Percentage
description: Contractually agreed minimum uptime percentage for this GDS connection as specified in the GDS connectivity SLA. Used for vendor performance monitoring and contract compliance reporting.
Unique surrogate identifier for the OTA partner record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the ota_partner master data product.

Indicates whether the OTA partner operates an affiliate or sub-distribution network through which third-party websites or agents can book on behalf of the OTA. True = affiliate network active; False = direct OTA platform only. Relevant for booking source attribution accuracy.

Standard commission percentage charged by the OTA partner on each confirmed booking, expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Foundational input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel profitability analysis.

Default cancellation policy type applied to bookings made through this OTA channel. Flexible = free cancellation up to 24-48 hours; Moderate = partial penalty; Strict = high penalty; Non_Refundable = NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) — no refund on cancellation. Impacts revenue management and guest experience.

Name of the channel manager or switch platform used to manage the technical connectivity to this OTA partner (e.g., Sabre SynXis, Siteminder, Amadeus iHotelier, RateGain). Relevant when connectivity is not direct API-to-API.

Commercial model governing how the OTA partner earns revenue. Net Rate = hotel sells at net, OTA marks up; Merchant = OTA collects full payment and remits net; Agency = OTA collects commission on hotel-collected payment; Retail = standard published rate with commission; Opaque = rate hidden from consumer until post-booking (e.g., Hotwire).

Frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the OTA partner or channel manager. Annual = yearly flat fee; Monthly = recurring monthly; Per_Booking = transactional fee per confirmed booking; One_Time = setup/onboarding fee only.

Annual or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the OTA partner or channel manager for maintaining the technical integration (API/XML/GDS switch). Expressed in USD. Used for total cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation and channel profitability reporting per USALI.

Technical protocol used for two-way inventory, rate, and availability (IRA) connectivity between the hotel's CRS/PMS and the OTA partner. XML = legacy XML push/pull; REST_API = modern RESTful API; GDS = Global Distribution System switch; SFTP = file-based batch; OTA_XML = OpenTravel Alliance XML standard; HTNG = HTNG web services.

Numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the completeness and quality of the hotel's content (photos, descriptions, amenities) on the OTA partner's platform. Higher scores correlate with improved search ranking and conversion rates. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.
Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner becomes legally binding and operational. Governs the start of commission obligations and rate parity clauses.

Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended contract. Used for contract renewal management and risk monitoring.

Unique reference number of the master distribution agreement or contract executed with the OTA partner. Links to the contract management repository in SAP S/4HANA for financial and legal audit purposes.
Timestamp when the OTA partner record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the OTA partner governing the exchange of guest booking data, anonymised analytics, and performance reporting. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance when guest PII is transmitted.

Indicates whether the OTA partner is formally designated as a GDPR Data Processor under Article 28, processing guest personal data on behalf of the hotel as Data Controller. True = Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed; False = not designated as processor.

Two-letter chain code assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) to identify the hotel brand/chain within GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Applicable only when connectivity_protocol is GDS. Null for non-GDS OTA partners.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the OTA partner's legal headquarters is registered. Used for tax treaty application, withholding tax determination, and regulatory jurisdiction identification.

Model governing how room inventory is made available to the OTA partner. Free_Sell = real-time availability from CRS with no pre-allocated block; Allotment = pre-contracted room block allocated to the OTA; On_Request = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis.

Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) clause, requiring the hotel to make its last available room accessible to this channel. LRA clauses impact revenue management inventory controls and are tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Full registered legal name of the OTA partner organisation as it appears on the master distribution agreement (e.g., Expedia, Inc.; Booking Holdings B.V.). Used for contract management, AP reconciliation, and regulatory filings.

Description of the primary geographic markets or regions where the OTA partner has significant consumer reach and booking volume (e.g., 'North America, Western Europe'; 'Asia-Pacific'; 'Global'). Used for channel mix optimisation and market-specific distribution strategy.

Indicates whether the OTA partner operates a dedicated mobile application channel through which bookings are made. True = mobile app channel is active and tracked separately for attribution; False = desktop/web only. Supports channel mix and mobile booking trend analysis.

Business email address of the OTA partner's designated account manager. Used for commercial communications, promotional campaign coordination, and escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.

Full name of the OTA partner's designated account manager or market manager assigned to the hotel account. Primary point of contact for commercial negotiations, promotional campaigns, and issue resolution.

Current lifecycle status of the OTA partner relationship. Active = live and distributing inventory; Pending = contract under negotiation or connectivity setup in progress; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity breach); Terminated = contract ended.

OTA = Online Travel Agency (Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (Sabre, Amadeus); METASEARCH = price comparison engine (Google Hotel Ads, Trivago); DIRECT = brand.com or call centre; WHOLESALE = tour operator/bed bank; AFFILIATE = sub-affiliate network. Identifies which party collects payment from the guest. Hotel = property collects at check-in/check-out (agency model); OTA = OTA collects full payment and remits net to hotel (merchant model); Split = partial payment collected by each party.
