Reference to the property for which this daily revenue performance record is captured. Links to the property master entity in the Property domain.

Average Daily Rate in the property's operating currency, calculated as total room revenue divided by rooms sold (excluding complimentary). Core KPI for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, ARI, and MPI calculations.
Total ancillary revenue on the performance date from upsells, packages, resort fees, and add-on services not captured in departmental revenue lines. Supports total revenue and TRevPAR reporting.

Average Rate Index representing the property's ADR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. ARI > 1.0 indicates the property is achieving a rate premium over its comp set.
Number of rooms sold through direct channels (brand website, call center, walk-in) on the performance date. Supports direct vs. indirect channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

business_glossary_term: Online Travel Agency (OTA) Channel Rooms Sold
description: Number of rooms sold through Online Travel Agency (OTA) channels (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) on the performance date. Supports channel distribution mix analysis and OTA commission cost management.
Cost Per Occupied Room on the performance date, calculated as total rooms department operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures operational efficiency of the rooms department per USALI.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this performance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency property portfolio reporting and consolidation.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Contribution
description: Property-level EBITDA contribution on the performance date, representing operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Used for ownership reporting, asset valuation, and management contract performance measurement.

Total Food and Beverage revenue generated on the performance date across all F&B outlets (restaurants, bars, banquets, in-room dining). Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA. Included in TRevPAR calculation per USALI.

Gross Operating Profit on the performance date, calculated as total operated department revenue minus total operated department expenses per USALI. Key profitability metric for ownership reporting and management contracts.

business_glossary_term: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency. Core KPI for ownership reporting and management fee calculations.

Indicates whether this daily performance actual record has been reconciled between Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. True = reconciled and approved for reporting; False = pending reconciliation. Supports financial close and SOX controls.
Total miscellaneous and rental income on the performance date including rentals, commissions, and other non-departmental income per USALI non-operating income classification.

Market Penetration Index representing the property's occupancy performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. MPI > 1.0 indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of market occupancy.

Daily occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%), calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Core KPI for the STR STAR Report, DOR, and ownership reporting. Stored as a raw ratio; display layer applies percentage formatting.
Total parking revenue generated on the performance date from valet, self-park, and garage operations. Classified under other operated departments per USALI and included in TRevPAR.

The calendar date (business date) for which this revenue performance actual record applies. Represents the stay date or operating day used in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and all KPI calculations.

business_glossary_term: Prior Year Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: RevPAR for the same calendar date in the prior year. Used for year-over-year (YOY) variance analysis in the DOR, STR STAR Report benchmarking, and ownership reporting.

Lifecycle status of this performance actual record indicating whether figures are preliminary (intra-day), final (end-of-day close), adjusted (post-close correction), or closed (period-locked). Supports audit and reconciliation workflows.

Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as ADR multiplied by occupancy rate (or total room revenue divided by rooms available). Primary rooms revenue KPI for ownership reporting, STR STAR Report, and RGI benchmarking.

Revenue Generation Index representing the property's RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. RGI > 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its comp set. Core competitive benchmarking KPI.

Total number of rooms available for sale on the performance date after deducting out-of-order (OOO) and out-of-service (OOS) rooms. Denominator for Occupancy Rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations per USALI and STR STAR Report standards.

Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary (no-charge) basis on the performance date. Tracked separately per USALI for accurate ADR and revenue reporting, as complimentary rooms are excluded from ADR calculation.
Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the performance date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Excluded from rooms available per USALI inventory standards.
Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the performance date, including complimentary rooms. Used to calculate Occupancy Rate (OCC) and Average Daily Rate (ADR) per USALI standards.
Number of rooms sold under negotiated contract rates (airline crew, long-stay corporate contracts) on the performance date. Supports contract segment revenue analysis and channel mix reporting.
Number of rooms sold to group accounts (MICE, corporate groups, tour groups) on the performance date. Supports group vs. transient mix analysis and group revenue contribution reporting per USALI.
Number of rooms sold to transient (individual/FIT) guests on the performance date. Supports segment-level revenue analysis and channel mix reporting per USALI market segmentation standards.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this performance actual record was sourced or reconciled (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS for rooms data, SAP S/4HANA for financial totals). Supports data lineage and reconciliation audits.
Total spa and wellness revenue generated on the performance date. Included in total property revenue and TRevPAR calculation per USALI other operated departments classification.

Total gross revenue across all operated departments and income streams on the performance date (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous + ancillary). Denominator basis for TRevPAR. Reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL per USALI.

Total gross room revenue generated on the performance date across all rate plans and channels, before deductions. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL. Numerator for ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI.

Total Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as total property revenue divided by rooms available. Measures total revenue productivity of the property beyond rooms. Core KPI for ownership and management reporting.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was last modified, including post-close adjustments or reconciliation corrections. Supports audit trail and change tracking.
Unique surrogate identifier for each daily rate and availability snapshot record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the rate_availability data product.

Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Rate availability snapshots distributed to OTAs and GDS must include the specific cancellation policy for rate shopping compliance and guest transparency. Channel managers need this link to ensure acc

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: rate_availability.occupancy_forecast_pct and demand_forecast_level are execution-specific snapshot values captured at the time of the availability record and are retained per the transactional table r

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Rate/availability snapshots are channel-specific - each channel sees different rates and inventory based on connectivity and contracts. Core distribution management requires tracking what each channel

Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied by IDeaS G3 RMS to derive the current BAR for this snapshot. DRRs define the algorithmic pricing logic (demand-based adjustments, day-of-week factors, lead-time curves) that drive dynamic pricing decisions.

Foreign key linking to property.gds_profile. Business justification: GDS rate availability records must reference the specific GDS profile to validate rate parity compliance and distribution status. Rate parity monitoring across GDS channels is a regulatory and contrac
Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this rate and availability snapshot applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.

Foreign key linking to revenue.rate_restriction. Business justification: rate_availability is a daily snapshot of rate and availability per property/room_type/rate_plan. The snapshot should reference the active rate_restriction record that is constraining availability (e.g
Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) governing the pricing for this snapshot. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS rate plan catalog.
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this rate and availability snapshot is captured. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.

Minimum number of days in advance a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date (e.g., 7-day advance purchase, 21-day advance purchase for NRR rates). A key rate restriction parameter managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Current open/closed availability status for this property, room type, rate plan, and date combination as managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS. 'open' = bookable; 'closed' = inventory blocked; 'stop_sell' = yield-driven closure; 'on_request' = requires manual confirmation; 'min_stay_restriction' = LOS restriction active; 'closed_to_arrival' = no new arrivals permitted on this date.

Number of rooms of this room type available for sale on the snapshot date at this property. Represents net sellable inventory after deducting out-of-order, out-of-service, and already-committed rooms. Core input for OCC (Occupancy Rate) and RevPAR calculations.

The Best Available Rate (BAR) for this room type, property, and stay date — the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate as determined by IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing engine. BAR is the anchor rate from which other rate plans are derived and is the primary rate distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and OTA channels.

Indicates whether new arrivals are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check in on this date but may continue an existing stay through it. A yield management restriction used to optimize multi-night booking patterns.

Indicates whether departures are blocked on the snapshot date for this room type and rate plan. When True, guests cannot check out on this date, forcing stays to extend through it. Used in conjunction with CTA restrictions to shape booking patterns during peak periods.
The date and time when this rate and availability snapshot record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ETL pipeline tracking.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all rate amounts (BAR, min, max, rack) for this record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international channel distribution via Sabre SynXis CRS.

Demand forecast classification for the snapshot date as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Drives dynamic pricing decisions and inventory restriction strategies. Levels range from very_low (deep discounting warranted) to very_high (maximum rate and stop-sell controls active).

The date and time from which this rate and availability record became active and was distributed to booking channels. Used for bi-temporal modeling of rate history and supports point-in-time rate reconstruction for revenue audits and dispute resolution.

The date and time until which this rate and availability record remains active. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for bi-temporal modeling and historical rate reconstruction. When a new rate update is received, the prior record's effective_until_timestamp is set to the new record's effective_from_timestamp.

The rate code as published on GDS (Global Distribution System) platforms (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport) via Sabre SynXis CRS. GDS rate codes follow standardized formats required for travel agent booking systems and corporate travel management programs.

Number of rooms on the snapshot date allocated to group blocks (MICE, corporate groups, tour operators) as managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus. Group block inventory is excluded from transient availability and tracked separately for yield optimization.

Indicates whether this rate plan includes bundled package components (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, airport transfer) beyond the base room charge. Package rates require component-level revenue allocation across USALI departments in Oracle OPERA PMS.

The date and time when this rate and availability record was last modified in the source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS). Represents the business event timestamp of the most recent pricing or availability change. Critical for real-time channel distribution synchronization and SLA monitoring.

Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan for the snapshot date. Used to prevent long-stay bookings at promotional rates during high-demand periods. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS as an upper LOS (Length of Stay) restriction.

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to qualify for this rate plan on the snapshot date. A key yield optimization restriction managed in IDeaS G3 RMS. LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions are used to maximize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring multi-night commitments.

The ceiling rate (maximum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Caps pricing during peak demand periods to maintain guest trust and brand integrity, and supports rate parity compliance across channels.

The floor rate (minimum sellable rate) for this room type, property, rate plan, and stay date as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the lowest price at which the room may be sold under any circumstance, enforcing revenue floor controls and preventing rate erosion.

Forecasted occupancy rate (OCC%) for the property on the snapshot date as projected by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this snapshot. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to validate pricing decisions and benchmark against STR STAR Report actuals.

The rate identifier as assigned by the primary OTA (Online Travel Agency) partner (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) for this rate plan and property combination. Used for rate parity monitoring, OTA reconciliation, and channel performance reporting.

Number of rooms of this room type that are out of order (OOO) on the snapshot date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Out-of-order rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and impact OCC denominator calculations per USALI standards.

Number of rooms booked (picked up) for the snapshot date since the previous snapshot capture. Pickup velocity is a key revenue management signal used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS to detect demand acceleration and trigger dynamic rate adjustments.

Indicates whether the current rate for this snapshot was manually overridden by a revenue manager, superseding the automated recommendation from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Manual overrides are tracked for revenue strategy audit and RMS model calibration.

Free-text or coded reason provided by the revenue manager when manually overriding the RMS-recommended rate. Populated only when pricing_override_flag is True. Used for revenue strategy review, RMS model improvement, and audit trail compliance.

The published full undiscounted rate (rack rate) for this room type and stay date. Serves as the reference rate for discount calculations, loyalty redemption valuations, and walk-in pricing. Typically the highest rate tier and the baseline for USALI revenue reporting.

Indicates whether this rate record is subject to rate parity obligations with OTA (Online Travel Agency) partners. When True, the rate must match or be lower than rates published on OTA channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and channel distribution management.

Alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS (e.g., BAR, CORP01, PKG_BB, NRR_ADV21). Used for channel distribution, GDS mapping, and OTA rate loading.

Classification of the rate plan governing this availability record. Determines pricing logic, cancellation policy, and channel eligibility. BAR (Best Available Rate) = dynamic public rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) = guaranteed availability; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) = prepaid non-cancellable; corporate = negotiated account rate; package = bundled inclusions; group = block rate; loyalty = member rate; promotional = limited-time offer; wholesale = OTA/tour operator net rate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|group|loyalty|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]

Identifies the Revenue Management System (RMS) that generated or last updated the pricing recommendation for this snapshot record. Supports data lineage tracking and system-of-record attribution for audit and reconciliation purposes.

The stay date (arrival night) for which this rate and availability record applies. Represents the business event date — the night a guest would occupy the room at the stated rate. Core dimension for RevPAR and ADR reporting.

The native record identifier from the originating source system (IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS) for this rate and availability record. Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation, reprocessing, and audit purposes.

Indicates whether this room type and rate plan combination has been closed to all new bookings on the snapshot date regardless of available inventory. A hard yield management control applied in IDeaS G3 RMS to protect inventory for higher-value bookings or group blocks.

Total physical room count for this room type at the property on the snapshot date. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations. Reflects the property's sellable capacity before any restrictions.

Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the property in which rate floor, ceiling, and absolute adjustment values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of '1111100' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.
The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.
The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.

Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.

Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.
Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.

The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property's maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.

The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property's minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.
Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Rule Reference ID
description: Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.
Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule's business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.
Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).

Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.

Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: occupancy_based|pickup_based|competitive_based|event_based|los_based|forecast_based — promote to reference product]
Cumulative number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule has been triggered and applied a pricing adjustment since its activation date. Used to assess rule utilization and yield optimization effectiveness.

Comparison operator applied to the trigger threshold value to determine whether the rule condition is met. For example, 'greater_than' with a threshold of 0.80 means the rule fires when OCC exceeds 80%.

Unit of measure for the trigger threshold value. Percentage applies to OCC-based rules; room_count applies to pickup-based rules; index_value applies to competitive benchmarking rules (RGI, MPI, ARI); currency_amount applies to ADR-based triggers; nights applies to LOS-based rules.

The numeric threshold at which this Dynamic Rate Rule fires. For occupancy-based rules, this is the OCC percentage (e.g., 0.80 = 80%). For pickup-based rules, this is the number of rooms picked up within the pickup window. For competitive-based rules, this is the RGI or ARI index value.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer ETL pipeline.
Incremental version counter tracking the number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule configuration has been modified. Supports audit trail and change management for pricing strategy governance.
Unique surrogate identifier for the negotiated rate agreement record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to event.event_account. Business justification: Negotiated rates are contracted with specific event accounts (corporate, consortia, national accounts). Essential for contract rate management, event planner rate loading, and account-specific pricing

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Negotiated rate loading is authorized by a specific channel contract. Linking revenue_negotiated_rate to channel_contract supports rate audit, contract compliance reporting, and ensures negotiated rat

Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Corporate rate contract management: negotiated rates are owned by corporate accounts in hospitality. Revenue managers link contracted rates to the corporate account entity for contract compliance trac

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are loaded to specific channels (GDS with specific chain codes, OTA corporate programs, direct booking engines). Rate loading and distribution tracking requires knowing whic

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements specify brand standard rate requirements (LRA eligibility, minimum rate floors, CRS connectivity). Negotiated rates loaded for franchised properties must comply with franchise agr

Foreign key linking to property.gds_profile. Business justification: Negotiated rates loaded to GDS (identified by gds_chain_code and iata_number in revenue_negotiated_rate) must reference the property's GDS profile for rate loading validation and distribution tracking

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are frequently contracted at brand or regional hierarchy level and apply to all properties within that node. Account managers and revenue teams need to track which hierarchy

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates include outlet benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, lounge access). Required for contract fulfillment, benefit tracking, outlet revenue attribution, guest entitlement ver

Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Corporate rate negotiation contracts specify which F&B menu is included (e.g., Executive Breakfast Menu). Contract management and rate loading processes require this link to validate menu availability

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Negotiated rates are classified by market segment (e.g., Corporate, Wholesale). revenue_negotiated_rate has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N negotiated_rate records → 1

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates often include tier-based eligibility or enhanced benefits. Business process: enforcing tier requirements for corporate rate access, common in negotiated contracts that offer

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Negotiated rates are sometimes combinable with specific loyalty promotions (e.g., a corporate rate that also qualifies for a current bonus-points campaign). Revenue managers need to track which promot
Reference to the property for which this negotiated rate agreement applies. A single negotiated rate may be scoped to one property or replicated per property for multi-property contracts.
Reference to the parent rate plan definition under which this negotiated rate is structured. Links to the rate plan master in Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.
Reference to the specific room type to which this negotiated rate applies. Null if the rate applies to all room types under the agreement.
Minimum number of days in advance a reservation must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates same-day booking is permitted.

The contracted nightly room rate (Average Daily Rate) agreed upon for this account. Represents the fixed or base rate per room per night in the specified currency. Core pricing field for revenue management and contract compliance.
Approval workflow status for this negotiated rate agreement. Rates must be approved by revenue management leadership before loading into CRS/GDS channels.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for distribution.
Date and time when this negotiated rate agreement received final approval for loading and distribution.

Comma-separated list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a structured string; detailed blackout records are managed in the rate_restriction product.
Indicates whether breakfast is included in the negotiated rate. Affects F&B (Food and Beverage) revenue allocation and rate value proposition reporting.
Commission percentage payable to the TMC, travel agency, or consortia for reservations booked under this negotiated rate. Expressed as a percentage of the negotiated rate amount.
Total number of room nights the account has committed to book under this negotiated rate agreement for the contract period. Basis for volume discount qualification and contract performance tracking.
Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) under which this negotiated rate is offered. Null for direct corporate accounts not affiliated with a consortia.
The date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and the rate is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended agreements. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
External contract or agreement reference number assigned by the account, TMC, or legal department. Used for contract management, audit, and dispute resolution.
The date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes contractually binding and the rate is available for booking. Aligns with the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
Date and time when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.
Two-letter GDS chain code used to identify the hotel brand/chain in Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, and Worldspan for rate loading and distribution of this negotiated rate.
IATA-assigned numeric identifier for the Travel Management Company (TMC) or travel agency associated with this negotiated rate. Used for commission tracking and GDS rate access validation.

Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the property to honor the contracted rate as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of demand or yield management controls.
Indicates whether reservations booked under this negotiated rate are non-refundable (NRR). Affects cancellation policy enforcement and revenue recognition timing.
Maximum number of consecutive nights allowed per reservation under this negotiated rate. Null if no upper limit is imposed.
Minimum number of consecutive nights required per reservation to qualify for this negotiated rate. Enforced at booking in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.
Minimum number of room nights the account is contractually committed to consume during the contract period. Used for volume tracking and contract compliance reporting.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or sales manager responsible for negotiating and managing this rate agreement. Used for accountability and performance tracking.

The percentage discount or premium of this negotiated rate relative to the Best Available Rate (BAR) at time of contract negotiation. Used by revenue management to assess rate competitiveness and yield impact.

Alphanumeric rate code used to identify and load this negotiated rate in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. This is the externally-known business identifier referenced in GDS/CRS rate loading and reservation transactions.
Indicates whether the negotiated rate amount is inclusive of applicable taxes. Critical for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and guest billing accuracy.
Date and time when the negotiated rate was successfully loaded into Sabre SynXis CRS and/or Oracle OPERA PMS for distribution. Null if loading has not yet occurred.
Current status of the rate loading process in Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS. Indicates whether the negotiated rate has been successfully distributed to all applicable booking channels.

Human-readable name describing the negotiated rate agreement, typically referencing the account or program name (e.g., 'Acme Corp 2025 Corporate Rate'). Used in reporting and rate management dashboards.
Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable and distributed through CRS/GDS channels.

Classification of the pricing structure for this negotiated rate. Fixed rates are static for the contract period; dynamic rates may fluctuate within agreed bounds; tiered rates vary by volume; per diem rates are government-mandated ceiling rates.

Native identifier of this negotiated rate record in the originating operational system (Oracle OPERA PMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Salesforce CRM). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
Date and time when this negotiated rate record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental ETL processing.
Unique surrogate identifier for each rate restriction record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the rate_restriction data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to reservation.cancellation_policy. Business justification: Revenue managers apply cancellation policy restrictions as part of yield management strategy. Rate restrictions must enforce specific cancellation terms (non-refundable, free cancellation windows) to

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: rate_restriction.demand_forecast_level is a STRING category label (e.g., HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) that is kept as an execution-specific label. However, the restriction record should also reference the specifi

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Restrictions (stop-sell, min LOS, CTA/CTD) are applied per distribution channel. Revenue managers close channels selectively based on demand - essential inventory control operation. Enables channel-sp

Reference to the group block associated with this restriction, when the restriction is applied to protect inventory for a specific group booking or MICE event. Null for transient/individual restrictions.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Rate restrictions can be scoped to market segments (e.g., restrict Leisure segment on peak dates). rate_restriction has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N rate_restrictio
Reference to the property (hotel/resort) to which this rate restriction applies. Enables property-level yield optimization and restriction management across the portfolio.

Reference to the rate plan to which this restriction applies. Links to the rate plan master in the CRS/RMS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, DRR). A restriction may apply to one or multiple rate plans; this field captures the primary rate plan association.

Reference to the specific room type to which this restriction applies. When null, the restriction applies to all room types under the associated rate plan. Enables granular inventory control by room category.

Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Supports NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase yield strategies. Null when restriction type is not ADVANCE_PURCHASE.

The date and time when this rate restriction was activated and distributed to connected channels (CRS, GDS, OTA). Represents the principal business event time — when the restriction became operationally effective in the distribution ecosystem. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that created this rate restriction record. Supports SOX financial controls audit trail and accountability for yield management decisions.
The date and time when this rate restriction record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the hurdle_rate_amount and any monetary thresholds defined in this restriction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI standards.

7-character binary string indicating which days of the week the restriction applies, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., '1111100' = Mon–Fri only, '0000011' = Sat–Sun only). Enables day-of-week yield strategies such as weekend minimum stay requirements.

Demand forecast classification at the time this restriction was created or last updated, as determined by the RMS (IDeaS G3 or Infor EzRMS). Provides context for why the restriction was applied and supports post-hoc yield strategy analysis.

Indicates the current distribution state of this restriction across connected channels via Sabre SynXis CRS. 'pending' = queued for distribution; 'distributed' = successfully pushed to all channels; 'failed' = distribution error; 'partial' = distributed to some but not all channels.

The calendar date from which this rate restriction becomes active and enforceable. Corresponds to the stay date (not booking date) unless the restriction type is ADVANCE_PURCHASE. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.
The last calendar date on which this rate restriction is enforceable (inclusive). Null indicates an open-ended restriction with no defined end. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.

Minimum acceptable rate (in the property's base currency) below which a booking will be rejected for this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A key RMS yield control used to protect ADR during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not HURDLE_RATE.

Indicates whether the effective date range is designated as a blackout period, during which the rate plan is completely closed to new bookings. Commonly applied during peak demand events, holidays, or sold-out periods.

Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check in on the restricted date, preventing short-stay bookings that fragment inventory during high-demand periods.

Indicates whether departures are blocked on the effective date(s) for this rate plan and room type. When True, guests cannot check out on the restricted date, used to extend stays through high-demand nights and maximize occupancy continuity.

Indicates whether members of the hotel's loyalty program are exempt from this restriction (e.g., LRA — Last Room Availability guarantee for elite members). Supports loyalty program administration and member benefit fulfillment.

The date and time when this restriction was last successfully distributed to external channels (OTA, GDS) via Sabre SynXis CRS. Used to monitor distribution latency and SLA compliance for rate parity management.

Maximum number of days prior to arrival within which a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Defines the booking window ceiling for advance purchase restrictions, preventing bookings made too far in advance at promotional rates.

Maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). Used to prevent long-stay bookings at discounted rates during high-demand periods. Null when restriction type is not MAX_LOS.

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this rate plan on the restricted date(s). A core yield optimization lever in revenue management. Null when restriction type is not MIN_LOS.

Free-text field for additional operational notes or context provided by the revenue manager regarding this restriction. May include references to specific events, competitive intelligence, or strategy rationale not captured in structured fields.

Indicates whether a revenue manager or front desk supervisor is permitted to manually override this restriction for individual bookings. When False, the restriction is hard-enforced and cannot be bypassed in the PMS or CRS.

Minimum staff authorization level required to override this restriction when override_allowed is True. Ensures appropriate governance over yield control exceptions. Null when override_allowed is False.

Numeric priority rank used to resolve conflicts when multiple restrictions apply to the same rate plan, room type, and date. Lower values indicate higher priority. Ensures deterministic restriction enforcement when overlapping rules exist in the RMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate restriction rule as defined in IDeaS G3 RMS or Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and distribution to OTA and GDS channels.

Human-readable name describing the rate restriction rule (e.g., 'Weekend Min 2-Night Stay', 'Holiday CTA Block'). Used by revenue managers and front desk staff to identify restrictions in PMS and RMS interfaces.

Free-text or coded explanation for why this restriction was applied (e.g., 'Peak demand event', 'Holiday blackout', 'Group block protection', 'RMS auto-recommendation'). Supports revenue strategy documentation and post-stay performance analysis.

Defines the level at which this restriction is applied: 'property' = entire property; 'room_type' = specific room category; 'rate_plan' = specific rate plan only; 'channel' = specific distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct). Determines how broadly the restriction is enforced across inventory.

Current lifecycle state of the rate restriction record. 'active' = currently enforced; 'inactive' = disabled but retained for history; 'pending' = scheduled but not yet effective; 'expired' = past end date; 'overridden' = manually superseded by a revenue manager.

Classification of the restriction mechanism applied. MIN_LOS = Minimum Length of Stay; MAX_LOS = Maximum Length of Stay; CTA = Closed to Arrival; CTD = Closed to Departure; ADVANCE_PURCHASE = minimum days before arrival required to book; BLACKOUT = date fully closed to booking; HURDLE_RATE = minimum acceptable rate threshold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MIN_LOS|MAX_LOS|CTA|CTD|ADVANCE_PURCHASE|BLACKOUT|HURDLE_RATE — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this restriction was system-recommended by the RMS (True) or manually created by a revenue manager (False). Enables analysis of RMS automation adoption rates and comparison of system vs. human yield decisions.

Seasonal classification code associated with this restriction (e.g., PEAK, SHOULDER, OFF_PEAK, HOLIDAY). Aligns restrictions with the property's seasonal demand calendar and supports STR STAR Report benchmarking analysis.

The native identifier of this restriction record in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, etc.). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the system of record.
Username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system process that last modified this rate restriction record. Supports audit trail and governance of yield management changes.

The date and time when this rate restriction record was last modified in the source system. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Unique surrogate identifier for each rate plan record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the revenue_rate_plan data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.accrual_rule. Business justification: Rate plans define which loyalty accrual rule governs points earning (e.g., a promotional rate plan earns 2x points per a specific accrual rule). The accrual_rule.eligible_rate_codes is free-text; this

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise agreements mandate specific rate plan requirements (brand standard rates, LRA participation, loyalty fee structures). Linking rate plans to the governing franchise agreement enables brand au

Foreign key linking to property.gds_profile. Business justification: GDS-eligible rate plans must be loaded and validated against the property's GDS profile (chain_code, gds_property_code, distribution settings). Revenue and distribution managers need this link for GDS

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Brand-wide and regional promotional rate plans (e.g., brand BAR, seasonal promotions) are defined at hierarchy node level and cascade to all properties in that node. Revenue managers need to identify

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Rate plans include F&B benefits (breakfast at specific outlet, dining credits). Required for package fulfillment, benefit tracking, revenue allocation between rooms and F&B departments per USALI stand

Foreign key linking to fnb.menu. Business justification: Rate plans reference specific menus for included meals (breakfast menu version, dinner package menu). Required for package rate construction, menu version control, guest entitlement verification at ou

business_glossary_term: Included Spa Treatment Menu Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to spa.treatment_menu. Business justification: Luxury hotel rate plans bundle spa treatment menu access as an amenity (parallel to existing included_menu_id for F&B). Revenue managers configure which spa treatment menu is included in a rate plan f

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Rate plans enforce tier-specific eligibility (loyalty_tier_required field exists). Business process: validating member tier qualification for rate plan access at booking time. Essential for tier-based

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: revenue_rate_plan.market_segment_code is a denormalized STRING reference to market_segment.segment_code. Normalizing this to a proper FK (market_segment_id → revenue.market_segment.market_segment_id)

Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: MICE revenue management requires rate plans specific to individual meeting spaces (rental rate tiers, minimum spend packages). Revenue managers price meeting spaces as distinct inventory — linking rat

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Rate plans are directly tied to loyalty promotions (e.g., 'Book this rate, earn 3x points'). Revenue managers and loyalty teams jointly configure rate plan–promotion pairings for campaign launches. Th

Foreign key linking to property.property_facility. Business justification: Fee-based facilities (spa, parking, fitness center, pool) have associated rate plans for ancillary revenue management. Revenue managers create facility-specific rate plans (day pass rates, package inc

Reference to the property or hotel at which this rate plan is applicable. Enables property-level rate plan management across a multi-property portfolio. A null value indicates a chain-wide or global rate plan applicable to all properties.
Reference to the specific room type or room category to which this rate plan applies. A null value indicates the rate plan applies to all room types at the associated property.

Minimum number of days prior to arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan. Common values are 7, 14, 21, or 30 days for advance purchase promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement.
Name or identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this rate plan for distribution. Supports SOX financial controls and internal audit requirements for rate authorization governance.

The timestamp when this rate plan was formally approved for activation and distribution. Distinct from created_timestamp; captures the authorization event in the rate plan lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The principal nightly room rate amount in the operating currency before any adjustments, taxes, or fees. Represents the rack rate or floor price for this rate plan. Used as the reference value for ADR (Average Daily Rate) and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) calculations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.
The last date on which new reservations can be made under this rate plan. After this date the rate plan is closed to new bookings even if the stay dates fall within the effective period.
The earliest date on which reservations can be made under this rate plan. Used to control promotional rate availability windows and prevent premature booking of seasonal or event-driven rates.

Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy associated with this rate plan (e.g., FLEX24H, NRR_NOREF, 48H_PENALTY). Determines guest refund eligibility and penalty charges upon cancellation. Critical for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) and advance purchase rate plans.

The percentage of the room rate payable as commission to the booking agent or OTA when is_commissionable is true. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Null when is_commissionable is false.
The timestamp when this rate plan record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the base_rate_amount and all monetary fields on this rate plan are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.

Percentage discount applied to the BAR (Best Available Rate) or rack rate to derive this rate plan's selling price. Used for corporate negotiated rates, loyalty member rates, and promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no discount from the reference rate.

Indicates whether this rate plan is subject to DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) adjustments by the revenue management system. When true, IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS may automatically adjust the rate based on demand forecasts, occupancy levels, and competitive benchmarking.

The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and bookable. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS to control rate availability windows. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.

The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended rate plan with no defined end date. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.

Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport. Controls rate visibility to travel agents using GDS booking tools.
Indicates whether a travel agent or OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission is payable on bookings made under this rate plan. Drives commission accrual and payment processing in SAP S/4HANA AP.

Indicates whether this rate plan carries LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, meaning the contracted rate must be honored even when only the last room in a category is available. Applicable to corporate negotiated rate contracts.

Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for a full refund upon cancellation within the policy window. False for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) plans; true for flexible BAR (Best Available Rate) plans.

The timestamp when this rate plan record was most recently updated in the source system. Used to detect changes for incremental ETL processing and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan qualify for loyalty program points accrual. Wholesale, opaque, and certain promotional rates are typically ineligible for points earning.

Minimum loyalty program membership tier required to access this rate plan. A value of 'none' indicates the rate is open to all guests regardless of loyalty status. Used to gate exclusive member rates.
Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan. Used to prevent extended stays at promotional or deeply discounted rates. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction.

Indicates the F&B (Food and Beverage) inclusions bundled with this rate plan. Room only includes no meals; bed and breakfast includes morning meal; half board includes breakfast and dinner; full board includes all three meals; all-inclusive covers meals, beverages, and selected activities.

Minimum number of nights a guest must book to qualify for this rate plan. A LOS (Length of Stay) restriction used in yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Booking.com, Expedia, or Agoda. Controls rate visibility on third-party booking platforms.

Secondary classification grouping rate plans by the market segment or distribution channel they serve. Public rates are available to all guests; negotiated rates are contracted with specific accounts; wholesale rates are sold through tour operators; group rates apply to block bookings; loyalty rates are exclusive to program members; employee rates are internal staff benefits.
The maximum rate above which this rate plan cannot be sold, used to cap pricing during peak demand periods for contracted or promotional rates. Null indicates no ceiling constraint.

The minimum rate below which this rate plan cannot be sold, regardless of dynamic pricing adjustments. Protects rate integrity and brand positioning. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS as a hard constraint in yield optimization algorithms.

Indicates the organizational scope at which this rate plan is defined and managed. Property-level plans apply to a single hotel; brand-level plans apply across a brand portfolio; chain-level plans apply across the entire company; global plans may span international markets.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rate plan across systems (e.g., BAR, CORP001, PKG-SPA). Used as the business key in IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate plan lookup and distribution. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.

Free-text description of the rate plan's terms, inclusions, and conditions as displayed to guests and booking agents. Includes details on what is included in the rate, any special conditions, and marketing messaging.

Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and revenue managers (e.g., 'Best Available Rate', 'AAA Member Rate', 'Advance Purchase 21-Day'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.

Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR (Best Available Rate) is the publicly available flexible rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees availability to contracted accounts; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) carries no cancellation flexibility; corporate rates are negotiated with companies; package rates bundle room with ancillary services; promotional rates are time-limited offers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.

Current lifecycle state of the rate plan. Active plans are available for booking; inactive plans are temporarily disabled; draft plans are under configuration; suspended plans are paused due to yield or compliance review; archived plans are retired and no longer bookable. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
Identifies the operational system of record from which this rate plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same rate plan exists in multiple systems.

The native identifier of this rate plan in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Oracle OPERA PMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.

Defines any day-of-week or date-specific restrictions on when stays under this rate plan are permitted. Weekday-only and weekend-only restrictions are common for corporate and leisure rates respectively; blackout dates exclude specific high-demand periods.

Indicates whether the base_rate_amount includes applicable taxes (VAT, occupancy tax, city tax). True for tax-inclusive markets; false for markets where taxes are added at checkout. Critical for accurate revenue recognition under USALI and IFRS/GAAP.
